channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Stellantis CEO under fire from Italian lawmakers as the group grapples with financial troubles
item.description: Italian lawmakers on Friday questioned the head of troubled French-Italian automaker Stellantis over the group’s strategy, one day after it announced a management shakeup in an effort to revive sales.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Treasury official says withdrawing the US from the IMF and World Bank would be a 'step backward'
item.description: U_S_ Treasury official Jay Shambaugh says withdrawing the U_S_ from the IMF and World Bank would be “a step backward.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New arrests revive concern that nonviolent political protesters in Myanmar may be tortured
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Concern was rising on Friday that two nonviolent activists opposed to military rule in Myanmar are at high risk of torture after being arrested in raids this week in Yangon, the country’s biggest city.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel prize for A-bomb survivors' group boosts hope for Japanese seeking a nuke-free world
item.description: For many Japanese survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and their relatives, Friday’s awarding of the Nobel Peace Prize to a Japanese organization against nuclear weapons gives them hope for a new momentum to push for a nuclear-free world.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bolton: Odds that Trump would withdraw from NATO very high
item.description: Former White House national security adviser John Bolton warned that former President Trump is likely to withdraw from NATO if reelected in November.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Russian and Iranian presidents meet as concerns grow over escalating Middle East attacks
item.description: ASHGABAT, Turkmenistan (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin met Iran’s president on Friday, at a time when Tehran is supplying weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine and concerns are growing over escalating attacks between Israel and Iran and its militant allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken tells ASEAN the US is worried about China's 'dangerous' actions in disputed sea.
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken told Southeast Asian leaders Friday that the U.S. is concerned about China's “increasingly dangerous and unlawful” activities in the disputed South China Sea during an annual summit meeting, and pledged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Archaeologists unearth well-preserved 4,000-year-old Egyptian tomb
item.description: A joint Egyptian-German archaeological mission found the tomb of an ancient governor's daughter. Two tombs were found, one inside the other, covered in intricate script and art.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Partial remains of British climber believed found 100 years after Everest ascent
item.description: Climbers believe they have found the partial remains of a British mountaineer who might — or might not — have been one of the first two people to climb Mount Everest, a century after their attempt on the world’s highest peak.
item.dc:creator: Pan Pylas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea accuses South Korea of flying drones to its capital and threatens to attack next time
item.description: North Korea has accused rival South Korea of flying drones to its capital to drop anti-North Korean propaganda leaflets and threatened to respond with force if such flights occur again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy appeals to allies to keep up aid as Germany pledges new weapons package
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says it’s important that allies’ aid to Ukraine doesn’t decrease next year.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in new, separate strikes
item.description: The Lebanese army says that an Israeli airstrike killed two of its soldiers and wounded three others, an incident that further entangled Lebanon's official army in the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dozens wounded, at least 22 killed in airstrikes in Beirut as tensions escalate in Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: At least 22 people were killed and dozens of others left wounded in airstrikes in central Beirut, further escalating Israel’s deadly conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A new mayor takes office in southern Mexico after his predecessor was beheaded
item.description: CHILPANCINGO, Mexico (AP) — A new mayor was sworn in Thursday in a city in southern Mexico where his predecessor was killed and beheaded less than a week after taking office.
item.dc:creator: ALEJANDRINO GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Brown votes against student proposal to divest from companies allegedly connected to war in Gaza
item.description: Brown University announced Thursday it will not divest from companies that pro-Palestinian students say contribute to Israel's war efforts in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission on Thursday accused Israel of destroying Gaza's health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas and said that Palestinian prisoners and Israeli hostages have been tortured and sexually abused.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel seeks to reshape Middle East, with force and US backing
item.description: One year after Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack, Israel is reshaping the Middle East by force, taking the fight to Iran and its proxies with the help of the United States — despite pleas from the Biden administration for diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mozambique counts votes in its presidential election as opposition alleges fraud
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique began counting votes late Wednesday in a presidential election that is expected to extend the ruling party's 49 years in power, though the opposition was already alleging fraud and manipulation.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Susan Rice: Trump's reported calls with Putin appear illegal
item.description: Former White House adviser and United Nations ambassador Susan Rice called out former President Trump over a report that he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin at least seven times since leaving office, suggesting such calls would violate the Logan Act.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID-19 tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic, as was revealed in a new book by journalist Bob Woodward, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: British doctor admits to attempted murder after injecting mother’s partner with poison disguised as vaccine
item.description: British doctor, Thomas Kwan, pleaded guilty to an attempted murder charge after 'overwhelming' evidence showed he administer a poisonous fake COVID vaccination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel defense minister cancels visit with Pentagon chief
item.description: Israel has canceled a planned Wednesday meeting between its Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin, the Pentagon confirmed Tuesday. “We were just informed that Minister Gallant will be postponing his trip to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation
